task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Change Volume Password' dialog box in VeraCrypt is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Change Volume Password' (or similar) is visible, typically accessed via the 'Volumes' menu in the VeraCrypt main window.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct volume file is selected in the dialog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file path '/home/ga/Volumes/test_volume.hc' is visible in the volume selection text box within the 'Change Volume Password' dialog.

task_completion.description: Verify that the current password has been entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Current Password' field is populated with text (either asterisks/dots or plain text if 'Display password' is checked).

task_completion.description: Verify that the new password has been entered and confirmed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Both the 'New Password' and 'Confirm Password' fields are populated with text (asterisks/dots or plain text).

task_completion.description: Verify that PIM and hash algorithm settings are left at their defaults.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Use PIM' checkbox is unchecked (or the PIM field is empty), and the Hash algorithm dropdown is left at its default value without being changed.

task_completion.description: Verify that the password change was applied and a success confirmation is shown.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'OK' button is clicked in the dialog, followed by a progress bar (Random Pool Enrichment/header rewriting) and/or a VeraCrypt success message box stating the password has been successfully changed.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the VeraCrypt GUI to perform the task, as requested.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show mouse clicks and typing within the VeraCrypt graphical interface, rather than terminal commands executing the password change.

integrity.description: Ensure the actual VeraCrypt application is being interacted with.
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard VeraCrypt window borders, icons, and UI elements are present, not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Ensure the password was not changed using CLI commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible showing 'veracrypt --change-password' or similar command-line bypasses.
